title: pilosella auriculoides subsp. svevora
intro: Pilosella auriculoides subsp. svevora, also known as Svevo's Mouse-ear Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The medicinal properties of Pilosella auriculoides subsp. svevora lie in its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable addition to traditional herbal remedies. Pilosella auriculoides subsp. svevora can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, including skin irritations and digestive issues.
